title: Wild Green World
subtitle: A First Look at Forest Animals
author: Hannah Whitfield
subject: forest animals and the leafy green places they live
flux_style: Soft hand-painted watercolour storybook illustration in an adorable, sweet kawaii style — large friendly sparkly eyes, a gentle happy face, soft edges and tender huggable charm — with every animal drawn in its OWN correct natural body shape and true proportions for its real species (flat animals stay flat, long animals stay long, never forced into a uniform round chubby ball), loose simplified naive childlike style, gentle softly muted warm forest palette of soft greens, warm browns and golden dappled sunlight, soft tender pastel watercolour washes, gentle visible brushwork, soft edges, painterly flat shapes, bright friendly, children's picture book art, not photorealistic, not a photograph, no text

art_prompt: a soft watercolour storybook front cover: ONE single adorable, joyful red fox cub as the BIG central hero of the cover, large and prominent and filling most of the frame, centred in the composition and taking up the majority of the picture, smiling a big warm happy smile with large sparkly friendly eyes and soft rosy cheeks, a fluffy warm orange coat and a big soft bushy tail, sitting cheerfully in a gentle green forest glade; soft green ferns and leafy trees and warm golden dappled sunlight, the background richly and evenly painted from top to bottom all the way to every edge with soft greens and warm light (no other animals, no people); the fox's head, ears, back and body are completely smooth, clean and bare with nothing sprouting from them; the fox looks happy, warm, gentle, sweet and inviting (a cheerful friendly smile, definitely NOT sad, NOT scary, NOT angry, NOT sleepy); exaggerated kawaii cuteness, the whole frame richly painted in gentle softly muted greens and golden light, bright cheerful and inviting, no washed-out or blank empty areas, no text, no words
blurb: Step into a wild green world! From a gentle spotted fawn in a sunny clearing to a bright red cardinal high in the leaves, this gentle first forest book takes early readers on a walk through the woods to meet twenty woodland animals in the leafy places they call home. Soft, glowing watercolours and simple, read-aloud rhymes make every page a warm discovery — a perfect first forest adventure for curious little ones.
